Aaron.title: Aaron

Belshazzar.summary: Bel protect the king!, the last of the kings of Babylon .

Benaiah.title: Benaiah
Benaiah.summary: The son of Jehoiada, chief priest .

Ben-ammi.title: Ben-ammi
Ben-ammi.summary: Son of my kindred; i.e., “born of incest”, the son of Lot by his youngest daughter .

Benjamin.title: Benjamin (patriarch)
Benjamin.summary: The younger son of Jacob by Rachel .

Beor.title: Beor
Beor.summary: The father of Bela, one of the kings of Edom .

Bera.title: Bera
Bera.summary: Gift, or son of evil, king of Sodom at the time of the invasion of the four kings under Chedorlaomer (, , , ).

Berachah.title: Berachah
Berachah.summary: One of the Benjamite warriors, Saul’s brethren, who joined David when at Ziklag .

Bered.title: Bered
Bered.summary: A son of Shuthelah, and grandson of Ephraim .

Beriah.title: Beriah
Beriah.summary: One of Asher’s four sons, and father of Heber .

Beriah.title: Beriah
Beriah.summary: A son of Ephraim , born after the slaughter of his brothers, and so called by his father “because it went evil with his house” at that time.

Beriah.title: Beriah
Beriah.summary: A Benjamite who with his brother Shema founded Ajalon and expelled the Gittites .

Bernice.title: Bernice
Bernice.summary: Bearer of victory, the eldest daughter of Agrippa I., the Herod Agrippa of .

Bethuel.title: Bethuel
Bethuel.summary: The son of Nahor by Milcah; nephew of Abraham, and father of Rebekah (, ; , , ).

Bezaleel.title: Bezaleel
Bezaleel.summary: In the shadow of God; i.e., “under his protection”, the artificer who executed the work of art in connection with the tabernacle in the wilderness .

Bildad.title: Bildad
Bildad.summary: Son of contention, one of Job’s friends.

Bilgah.title: Bilgah
Bilgah.summary: The head of the fifteenth sacerdotal course for the temple service .

Bilgah.title: Bilgah
Bilgah.summary: A priest who returned from Babylon with Zerubbabel .

Bilhah.title: Bilhah
Bilhah.summary: Faltering; bashful, Rachel’s handmaid, whom she gave to Jacob .

Bilshan.title: Bilshan
Bilshan.summary: Son of the tongue; i.e., “eloquent”, a man of some note who returned from the Captivity with Zerubbabel .

Birsha.title: Birsha
Birsha.summary: Son of wickedness, a king of Gomorrah whom Abraham succoured in the invasion of Chedorlaomer .

Blastus.title: Blastus
Blastus.summary: Chamberlain to king Herod Agrippa I.

Boaz.title: Boaz
Boaz.summary: The husband of Ruth, a wealthy Bethlehemite.

Buz.title: Buz
Buz.summary: The second son of Nahor and Milcah, and brother of Huz .

Buz.title: Buz
Buz.summary: One of the chiefs of the tribe of Gad .

Cain.title: Cain
Cain.summary: The first-born son of Adam and Eve .

Cainan.title: Cainan (antediluvian patriarch)
Cainan.summary: The fourth antediluvian patriarch, the eldest son of Enos.

Calcol.title: Calcol
Calcol.summary: , sustenance, the same probably as Chalcol , one of the four sages whom Solomon excelled in wisdom; for “he was wiser than all men.”

Caleb.title: Caleb
Caleb.summary: One of the three sons of Hezron of the tribe of Judah.

Candace.title: Candace
Candace.summary: The queen of the Ethiopians whose “eunuch” or chamberlain was converted to Christianity by the instrumentality of Philip the evangelist .

Carmi.title: Carmi
Carmi.summary: The last named of the four sons of Reuben .

Carmi.title: Carmi
Carmi.summary: The son of Zimri, and the father of Achan , “the troubler of Israel.”

Casluhim.title: Casluhim
Casluhim.summary: Fortified, a people descended from Mizraim .

Chedorlaomer.title: Chedorlaomer
Chedorlaomer.summary: (= Khudur-Lagamar of the inscriptions), king of Elam.

Chemosh.title: Chemosh
Chemosh.summary: The destroyer, subduer, or fish-god, the god of the Moabites (; , , ).

Chilion.title: Chilion
Chilion.summary: The pining one, the younger son of Elimelech and Naomi, and husband of Orpah, Ruth’s sister .

Chloe.title: Chloe
Chloe.summary: Verdure, a female Christian , some of whose household had informed Paul of the divided state of the Corinthian church.

Chushan-rishathaim.title: Chushan-rishathaim
Chushan-rishathaim.summary: Cush of double wickedness, or governor of two presidencies, the king of Mesopotamia who oppressed Israel in the generation immediately following Joshua .

Clement.title: Clement
Clement.summary: Mild, a Christian of Philippi, Paul’s “fellow-labourer,” whose name he mentions as “in the book of life” .

Cleophas.title: Cleophas
Cleophas.summary: (abbreviation of Cleopatros), one of the two disciples with whom Jesus conversed on the way to Emmaus on the day of the resurrection .

Cornelius.title: Cornelius
Cornelius.summary: A centurion whose history is narrated in .

Crescens.title: Crescens
Crescens.summary: Increasing, probably one of the seventy disciples of Christ.

Crispus.title: Crispus
Crispus.summary: Curled, the chief of the synagogue at Corinth .

Cush.title: Cush (son of Ham)
Cush.summary: A son, probably the eldest, of Ham, and the father of Nimrod .

Dagon.title: Dagon
Dagon.summary: Little fish; diminutive from dag = a fish, the fish-god; the national god of the Philistines .

Damaris.title: Damaris
Damaris.summary: A heifer, an Athenian woman converted to Christianity under the preaching of Paul .

Daniel.title: Daniel
Daniel.summary: David’s second son, “born unto him in Hebron, of Abigail the Carmelitess” .

Daniel.title: Daniel
Daniel.summary: Beltis protect the king!, the Chaldee name given to Daniel by Nebuchadnezzar .

Darda.title: Darda
Darda.summary: Pearl of wisdom, one of the four who were noted for their wisdom, but whom Solomon excelled .

Darius.title: Darius
Darius.summary: Darius the Persian  was probably the Darius II.

Dathan.title: Dathan
Dathan.summary: Welled; belonging to a fountain, a son of Eliab, a Reubenite, who joined Korah (q.v.) in his conspiracy, and with his accomplices was swallowed up by an earthquake .

David.title: David
David.summary: Beloved, the eighth and youngest son of Jesse, a citizen of Bethlehem.

Debir.title: Debir
Debir.summary: The king of Eglon, one of the five Canaanitish kings who were hanged by Joshua  after the victory at Gibeon.

Dedan.title: Dedan (son of Jokshan)
Dedan.summary: A son of Jokshan, Abraham’s son by Keturah .

Delaiah.title: Delaiah
Delaiah.summary: The head of the twenty-third division of the priestly order .

Delaiah.title: Delaiah
Delaiah.summary: The head of one of the bands of exiles that returned under Zerubbabel to Jerusalem .

Delilah.title: Delilah
Delilah.summary: Languishing, a Philistine woman who dwelt in the valley of Sorek .

Demas.title: Demas
Demas.summary: A companion and fellow-labourer of Paul during his first imprisonment at Rome (:24; ).

Demetrius.title: Demetrius
Demetrius.summary: A silversmith at Ephesus, whose chief occupation was to make “silver shrines for Diana” (q.v.), , i.e., models either of the temple of Diana or of the statue of the goddess.

Diana.title: Diana
Diana.summary: So called by the Romans; called Artemis by the Greeks, the “great” goddess worshipped among heathen nations under various modifications.

Diblaim.title: Diblaim
Diblaim.summary: Doubled cakes, the mother of Gomer, who was Hosea’s wife .

Dinah.title: Dinah
Dinah.summary: Judged; vindicated, daughter of Jacob by Leah, and sister of Simeon and Levi .

Dionysius.title: Dionysius
Dionysius.summary: The Areopagite, one of Paul’s converts at Athens .

Dishan.title: Dishan
Dishan.summary: Antelope, the youngest son of Seir the Horite, head of one of the tribes of Idumaea (, , ).

Dodo.title: Dodo
Dodo.summary: An Ahohite, father of Eleazar, who was one of David’s three heroes .

Dodo.title: Dodo
Dodo.summary: A Bethlehemite, and father of Elhanan, who was one of David’s thirty heroes .

Doeg.title: Doeg
Doeg.summary: Fearful, an Edomite, the chief overseer of Saul’s flocks .

Dorcas.title: Dorcas
Dorcas.summary: (in Greek called Dorcas), gazelle, a disciple at Joppa.

Drusilla.title: Drusilla
Drusilla.summary: Third and youngest daughter of Herod Agrippa I.

Ebal.title: Ebal (son of Shobal)
Ebal.summary: A descendant of Seir the Horite .

Ebal.title: Ebal (son of Joktan)
Ebal.summary: A descendant of Eber , called also Obal .

Ebed.title: Ebed
Ebed.summary: Slave, the father of Gaal, in whom the men of Shechem “put confidence” in their conspiracy against Abimelech (, , , ).

Eber.title: Eber (Heber)
Eber.summary: The third post-duluvian patriach after Shem .

Eber.title: Eber (a Benjamite)
Eber.summary: The oldest of the three sons of Elpaal the Benjamite .

Eber.title: Eber (Amok Family Head)
Eber.summary: The head of the priestly family of Amok in the time of Zerubbabel .

Ebiasaph.title: Ebiasaph
Ebiasaph.summary: Father of gathering; the gatherer, the youngest of the three sons of Korah the Levite, head of a family of Korhites ; called Ebisaph .

Eden.title: Eden
Eden.summary: Son of Joah, and one of the Levites who assisted in reforming the public worship of the sanctuary in the time of Hezekiah .

Eder.title: Eder
Eder.summary: The second of the three sons of Mushi, of the family of Merari, appointed to the Levitical office .

Eglah.title: Eglah
Eglah.summary: A heifer, one of David’s wives, and mother of Ithream .

Eglon.title: Eglon
Eglon.summary: Chieftain or king of one of the Moabite tribes .

Ehud.title: Ehud
Ehud.summary: The son of Gera, of the tribe of Benjamin .

Ehud.title: Ehud
Ehud.summary: A descendant of Benjamin , his great-grandson.

Elah.title: Elah
Elah.summary: One of the Edomite chiefs or “dukes” of Mount Seir .

Elah.title: Elah
Elah.summary: The son and successor of Baasha, king of Israel .

Elam.title: Elam (son of Shem)
Elam.summary: Highland, the son of Shem , and the name of the country inhabited by his descendants (14:1, 9; ; , etc.) lying to the east of Babylonia, and extending to the shore of the Mediterranean, a distance in a direct line of about 1,000 miles.

Elasah.title: Elasah
Elasah.summary: The son of Shaphan, one of the two who were sent by Zedekiah to Nebuchadnezzar, and also took charge of Jeremiah’s letter to the captives in Babylon .

Eldad.title: Eldad
Eldad.summary: Whom God has loved, one of the seventy elders whom Moses appointed  to administer justice among the people.

Eleazar.title: Eleazar
Eleazar.summary: An inhabitant of Kirjath-jearim who was “sanctified” to take charge of the ark, although not allowed to touch it, while it remained in the house of his father Abinadab (, ; comp.

Eleazar.title: Eleazar
Eleazar.summary: The son of Dodo the Ahohite, of the tribe of Benjamin, one of the three most eminent of David’s thirty-seven heroes  who broke through the Philistine host and brought him water from the well of Bethlehem .

Elhanan.title: Elhanan
Elhanan.summary: A warrior of the time of David famed for his exploits.

Elhanan.title: Elhanan
Elhanan.summary: The son of Dodo, and one of David’s warriors .

Eli.title: Eli
Eli.summary: Ascent, the high priest when the ark was at Shiloh .

Eliab.title: Eliab
Eliab.summary: A son of Helon, and chief of the tribe of Zebulun at the time of the census in the wilderness .

Eliada.title: Eliada
Eliada.summary: One of David’s sons born after his establishment in Jerusalem .

Eliada.title: Eliada
Eliada.summary: A mighty man of war, a Benjamite .

Eliakim.title: Eliakim
Eliakim.summary: The son of Hilkiah, who was sent to receive the message of the invading Assyrians and report it to Isaiah .

Eliakim.title: Eliakim
Eliakim.summary: The son of Abiud, of the posterity of Zerubbabel .

Eliam.title: Eliam
Eliam.summary: The father of Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah .

Eliam.title: Eliam
Eliam.summary: This name also occurs as that of a Gilonite, the son of Ahithophel, and one of David’s thirty warriors .

Eliashib.title: Eliashib
Eliashib.summary: A priest, head of one of the courses of the priests of the time of David .

Eliashib.title: Eliashib
Eliashib.summary: A high priest in the time of Ezra and Nehemiah .

Eliathah.title: Eliathah
Eliathah.summary: To whom God will come, one of the foureen sons of the Levite Heman, and musician of the temple in the time of David .

Elidad.title: Elidad
Elidad.summary: Whom God has loved, son of Chislon, and chief of the tribe of Benjamin; one of those who were appointed to divide the Promised Land among the tribes .

Eliel.title: Eliel
Eliel.summary: A chief of Manasseh, on the east of Jordan .

Eliel.title: Eliel
Eliel.summary: A Gadite who joined David in the hold at Ziklag .

Eliezer.title: Eliezer
Eliezer.summary: One of the two sons of Moses, born during his sojourn in Midian (; , ).

Eliezer.title: Eliezer
Eliezer.summary: The son of Becher, and grandson of Benjamin .

Elihu.title: Elihu
Elihu.summary: The son of Tohu, and grandfather of Elkanah .

Elihu.title: Elihu
Elihu.summary: One of the captains of thousands of Manasseh who joined David at Ziklag .

Elihu.title: Elihu
Elihu.summary: One of the family of Obed-edom, who were appointed porters of the temple under David .

Elijah.title: Elijah
Elijah.summary: “The Tishbite,” the “Elias” of the New Testament, is suddenly introduced to our notice in as delivering a message from the Lord to Ahab.

Elika.title: Elika
Elika.summary: God is his rejector, one of David’s thirty-seven distinguished heros .

Elimelech.title: Elimelech
Elimelech.summary: God his king, a man of the tribe of Judah, of the family of the Hezronites, and kinsman of Boaz, who dwelt in Bethlehem in the days of the judges.

Eliphalet.title: Eliphalet
Eliphalet.summary: God his deliverance, one of David’s sons ; called also Eliphelet .

Eliphaz.title: Eliphaz
Eliphaz.summary: The son of Esau by his wife Adah, and father of several Edomitish tribes (, , , ).

Elisabeth.title: Elisabeth
Elisabeth.summary: God her oath, the mother of John the Baptist .

Elisha.title: Elisha
Elisha.summary: God his salvation, the son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah, who became the attendant and disciple of Elijah .

Elishah.title: Elishah
Elishah.summary: The oldest of the four sons of Javan , whose descendants peopled Greece.

Elishama.title: Elishama
Elishama.summary: A prince of Benjamin, grandfather of Joshua .

Elishaphat.title: Elishaphat
Elishaphat.summary: Whom God has judged, one of the “captains of hundreds” associated with Jehoiada in the league to overthrow the usurpation of Athaliah .

Elisheba.title: Elisheba
Elisheba.summary: God is her oath, the daughter of Amminadab and the wife of Aaron .

Elishua.title: Elishua
Elishua.summary: God his salvation, a son of David, = Elishama, .

Elkanah.title: Elkanah
Elkanah.summary: The second son of Korah , or, according to , , more correctly his grandson.

Elkanah.title: Elkanah
Elkanah.summary: Another Levite of the line of Heman the singer, although he does not seem to have performed any of the usual Levitical offices.

Elon.title: Elon
Elon.summary: A Hittite, father of Bashemath, Esau’s wife .

Elon.title: Elon
Elon.summary: One of the sons of Zebulun .

Elon.title: Elon
Elon.summary: The eleventh of the Hebrew judges.

Elymas.title: Elymas
Elymas.summary: Magician or sorcerer, the Arabic name of the Jew Bar-jesus, who withstood Paul and Barnabas in Cyprus.

Enoch.title: Enoch (son of Cain)
Enoch.summary: The eldest son of Cain , who built a city east of Eden in the land of Nod, and called it “after the name of his son Enoch.” This is the first “city” mentioned in Scripture.

Enoch.title: Enoch (father of Methuselah)
Enoch.summary: The son of Jared, and father of Methuselah .

Enos.title: Enos
Enos.summary: Man the son of Seth, and grandson of Adam .

Epaphras.title: Epaphras
Epaphras.summary: Lovely, spoken of by Paul  as “his dear fellow-servant,” and “a faithful minister of Christ.” He was thus evidently with him at Rome when he wrote to the Colossians.

Epher.title: Epher
Epher.summary: One of the sons of Midian, who was Abraham’s son by Keturah .

Epher.title: Epher
Epher.summary: The head of one of the families of trans-Jordanic Manasseh who were carried captive by Tiglath-pileser .

Ephraim.title: Ephraim
Ephraim.summary: Double fruitfulness (“for God had made him fruitful in the land of his affliction”).

Ephratah.title: Ephratah
Ephratah.summary: The second wife of Caleb, the son of Hezron, mother of Hur, and grandmother of Caleb, who was one of those that were sent to spy the land .

Ephron.title: Ephron
Ephron.summary: The son of Zohar a Hittite, the owner of the field and cave of Machpelah (q.v.), which Abraham bought for 400 shekels of silver (; ; , ).

Erastus.title: Erastus
Erastus.summary: A companion of Paul at Ephesus, who was sent by him along with Timothy into Macedonia .

Eshcol.title: Eshcol
Eshcol.summary: A young Amoritish chief who joined Abraham in the recovery of Lot from the hands of Chedorlaomer .

Esther.title: Esther
Esther.summary: The queen of Ahasuerus, and heroine of the book that bears her name.

Ethan.title: Ethan
Ethan.summary: A Levite of the family of Gershom , probably the same as Ethan (42).

Ethbaal.title: Ethbaal
Ethbaal.summary: With Baal, a king of Sidon (B.C.

Euodias.title: Euodias
Euodias.summary: A good journey, a female member of the church at Philippi.

Eutychus.title: Eutychus
Eutychus.summary: Fortunate, , a young man of Troas who fell through drowsiness from the open window of the third floor of the house where Paul was preaching, and was “taken up dead.” The lattice-work of the window being open to admit the air, the lad fell out and down to the court below.

Eve.title: Eve
Eve.summary: Life; living, the name given by Adam to his wife .

Evil-merodach.title: Evil-merodach
Evil-merodach.summary: Merodach’s man, the son and successor of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon (; , ).

Ezekiel.title: Ezekiel
Ezekiel.summary: One of the great prophets, the son of Buzi the priest .

Ezer.title: Ezer
Ezer.summary: One of the sons of Seir, the native princes, “dukes,” of Mount Hor .

Ezra.title: Ezra
Ezra.summary: The “scribe” who led the second body of exiles that returned from Babylon to Jerusalem B.C.

Ezra.title: Ezra
Ezra.summary: A priest among those that returned to Jerusalem under Zerubabel .

Ezri.title: Ezri
Ezri.summary: Help of Jehovah, the son of Chelub.

Felix.title: Felix
Felix.summary: Happy, the Roman procurator of Judea before whom Paul “reasoned” .

Fortunatus.title: Fortunatus
Fortunatus.summary: Fortunate, a disciple of Corinth who visited Paul at Ephesus, and returned with Stephanas and Achaicus, the bearers of the apostle’s first letter to the Corinthians .

Gaal.title: Gaal
Gaal.summary: Loathing, the son of Ebed, in whom the Shechemites “placed their confidence” when they became discontented with Abimelech.

Gabriel.title: Gabriel
Gabriel.summary: Champion of God, used as a proper name to designate the angel who was sent to Daniel (8:16) to explain the vision of the ram and the he-goat, and to communicate the prediction of the seventy weeks .

Gad.title: Gad
Gad.summary: Jacob’s seventh son, by Zilpah, Leah’s handmaid, and the brother of Asher (; , ).

Gad.title: Gad
Gad.summary: A prophet who joined David in the “hold,” and at whose advice he quitted it for the forest of Hareth .

Gaddi.title: Gaddi
Gaddi.summary: Fortunate, the representative of the tribe of Manasseh among the twelve “spies” sent by Moses to spy the land .

Gaddiel.title: Gaddiel
Gaddiel.summary: Fortune (i.e., sent) of God, the representative of the tribe of Zebulum among the twelve spies .

Gaius.title: Gaius (of Macedonia)
Gaius.summary: A Macedonian, Paul’s fellow-traveller, and his host at Corinth when he wrote his Epistle to the Romans (16:23).

Gaius.title: Gaius (of Derbe)
Gaius.summary: A man of Derbe who accompanied Paul into Asia on his last journey to Jerusalem

Gaius.title: Gaius
Gaius.summary: A Macedonian, Paul’s fellow-traveller, and his host at Corinth when he wrote his Epistle to the Romans (16:23).

Gallio.title: Gallio
Gallio.summary: The elder brother of Seneca the philosopher, who was tutor and for some time minister of the emperor Nero.

Gamaliel.title: Gamaliel
Gamaliel.summary: A chief of the tribe of Manasseh at the census at Sinai (; ; , ).

Gamaliel.title: Gamaliel
Gamaliel.summary: The son of rabbi Simeon, and grandson of the famous rabbi Hillel.

Gamul.title: Gamul
Gamul.summary: Weaned the leader of one of the priestly courses .

Gareb.title: Gareb
Gareb.summary: One of David’s warriors , an Ithrite.

Geber.title: Geber
Geber.summary: A valiant man, , one of Solomon’s purveyors, having jurisdiction over a part of Gilead, comprising all the kingdom of Sihon and part of the kingdom of Og .

Gedaliah.title: Gedaliah
Gedaliah.summary: The son of Ahikam, and grandson of Shaphan, secretary of king Josiah .

Gehazi.title: Gehazi
Gehazi.summary: Valley of vision, Elisha’s trusted servant (; ; , ).

Gemariah.title: Gemariah
Gemariah.summary: The son of Hilkiah, who accompanied Shaphan with the tribute-money from Zedekiah to Nebuchadnezzar, and was the bearer at the same time of a letter from Jeremiah to the Jewish captives at Babylon .

Gemariah.title: Gemariah
Gemariah.summary: The son of Shaphan, and one of the Levites of the temple in the time of Jehoiakim .

Genubath.title: Genubath
Genubath.summary: Theft, the son of Hadad, of the Edomitish royal family.

Gera.title: Gera
Gera.summary: The father of Ehud the judge .

Gera.title: Gera
Gera.summary: The father of Shimei, who so grossly abused David (; , ).

Gershom.title: Gershom
Gershom.summary: The elder of the two sons of Moses born to him in Midian .

Gershom.title: Gershom
Gershom.summary: The son of Manasseh , in R.V.

Gershom.title: Gershom
Gershom.summary: A descendant of Phinehas who returned with Ezra from Babylon .

Gershon.title: Gershon
Gershon.summary: '=Ger’shom expulsion, the eldest of Levi’s three sons .

Gideon.title: Gideon
Gideon.summary: Called also Jerubbaal , was the first of the judges whose history is circumstantially narrated (-8).

Gog.title: Gog
Gog.summary: A Reubenite , the father of Shimei.

Goliath.title: Goliath
Goliath.summary: A famous giant of Gath, who for forty days openly defied the armies of Israel, but was at length slain by David with a stone from a sling .

Gomer.title: Gomer (son of Japheth)
Gomer.summary: The eldest son of Japheth, and father of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah , whose descendants formed the principal branch of the population of South-eastern Europe.

Hadad.title: Hadad
Hadad.summary: An Edomite king who defeated the Midianites .

Hadad.title: Hadad
Hadad.summary: One of “the king’s seed in Edom.” He fled into Egypt, where he married the sister of Pharaoh’s wife .

Hadad.title: Hadad
Hadad.summary: One of the Edomitish kings  about the time of Saul.

Hadar.title: Hadar
Hadar.summary: A son of Ishmael ; in written Hadad.

Hadarezer.title: Hadarezer
Hadarezer.summary: Hadad is help; called also Hadarezer, Adod is his help, the king of Zobah.

Hadlai.title: Hadlai
Hadlai.summary: Resting, an Ephraimite; the father of Amasa, mentioned in .

Hadoram.title: Hadoram
Hadoram.summary: The fifth son of Joktan, the founder of an Arab tribe .

Hagar.title: Hagar
Hagar.summary: Flight, or, according to others, stranger, an Egyptian, Sarah’s handmaid (; , ), whom she gave to Abraham (q.v.) as a secondary wife (16:2).

Haggai.title: Haggai
Haggai.summary: Festive, one of the twelve so-called minor prophets.

Haggith.title: Haggith
Haggith.summary: Festive; the dancer, a wife of David and the mother of Adonijah (; , ; 2:13; ), who, like Absalom, was famed for his beauty.

Hakkoz.title: Hakkoz
Hakkoz.summary: The thorn, the head of one of the courses of the priests .

Ham.title: Ham
Ham.summary: Warm, hot, and hence the south; also an Egyptian word meaning “black”, the youngest son of Noah (; comp.

Haman.title: Haman
Haman.summary: (of Persian origin), magnificent, the name of the vizier (i.e., the prime minister) of the Persian king Ahasuerus (, etc.).

Hammedatha.title: Hammedatha
Hammedatha.summary: Father of Haman, designated usually “the Agagite” (, ; ).

Hammelech.title: Hammelech
Hammelech.summary: The king’s, the father of Jerahmeel, mentioned in .

Hamor.title: Hamor
Hamor.summary: He-ass, a Hivite from whom Jacob purchased the plot of ground in which Joseph was afterwards buried .

Hamul.title: Hamul
Hamul.summary: Spared, one of the sons of Pharez, son of Judah .

Hamutal.title: Hamutal
Hamutal.summary: Kinsman of the dew, the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah, wife of king Josiah, and mother of king Jehoahaz , also of king Zedekiah .

Hanameel.title: Hanameel
Hanameel.summary: Whom God has graciously given, the cousin of Jeremiah, to whom he sold the field he possessed in Anathoth, before the siege of Jerusalem .

Hanani.title: Hanani

Kedar.title: Kedar
Kedar.summary: Dark-skinned, the second son of Ishmael .

Kedemah.title: Kedemah

Kemuel.title: Kemuel

Keren-happuch.title: Keren-happuch

Laban.title: Laban

Lemuel.title: Lemuel

Lud.title: Lud

Lysanias.title: Lysanias

Maachah.title: Maachah

Maath.title: Maath

Mamre.title: Mamre

Miriam.summary: , one of the descendants of Judah.

Moab.title: Moab

Phanuel.summary: Face of God, father of the prophetess Anna (q.v.), .

Pharaoh.title: Pharaoh (during Abraham's Journey)
Pharaoh.summary: The Pharaoh who was on the throne when Abram went down into Egypt  was probably one of the Hyksos, or “shepherd kings.” The Egyptians called the nomad tribes of Syria Shasu, “plunderers,” their king or chief Hyk, and hence the name of those invaders who conquered the native kings and established a strong government, with Zoan or Tanis as their capital.

Pharaoh.title: Pharaoh (of Joseph)
Pharaoh.summary: The Pharaoh of Joseph’s days  was probably Apopi, or Apopis, the last of the Hyksos kings.

Pharaoh.title: Pharaoh
Pharaoh.summary: The “new king who knew not Joseph”  has been generally supposed to have been Aahmes I., or Amosis, as he is called by Josephus.

Phebe.title: Phebe
Phebe.summary: A “deaconess of the church at Cenchrea,” the port of Corinth.

Phichol.title: Phichol
Phichol.summary: Great, the chief captain of the army of Abimelech, the Philistine king of Gerar.

Philemon.title: Philemon
Philemon.summary: An inhabitant of Colosse, and apparently a person of some note among the citizens (; :2).

Philetus.title: Philetus
Philetus.summary: Amiable, with Hymenaeus, at Ephesus, said that the “resurrection was past already” .

Philip.title: Phillip the Apostle
Philip.summary: One of the twelve apostles; a native of Bethsaida, “the city of Andrew and Peter” .

Philip.title: Philip
Philip.summary: Mentioned only in connection with the imprisonment of John the Baptist .

Phinehas.title: Phinehas (Levite)
Phinehas.summary: Son of Eleazar, the high priest .

Phinehas.title: Phinehas
Phinehas.summary: One of the sons of Eli, the high priest .

Phlegon.title: Phlegon
Phlegon.summary: Burning, a Roman Christian to whom Paul sent salutations .

Phygellus.title: Phygellus
Phygellus.summary: Fugitive, a Christian of Asia, who “turned away” from Paul during his second imprisonment at Rome .

Pontius.title: Pontius Pilate
Pontius.summary: Probably connected with the Roman family of the Pontii, and called “Pilate” from the Latin pileatus, i.e., “wearing the pileus”, which was the “cap or badge of a manumitted slave,” as indicating that he was a “freedman,” or the descendant of one.

Potiphar.title: Potiphar
Potiphar.summary: Dedicated to Ra; i.e., to the sun-god, the Egyptian to whom the Ishmaelites sold Joseph .

Priscilla.title: Priscilla
Priscilla.summary: The wife of Aquila , who is never mentioned without her.

Puah.title: Puah
Puah.summary: One of the two midwives who feared God, and refused to kill the Hebrew male children at their birth .

Publius.title: Publius
Publius.summary: “the chief man of the island” of Malta , who courteously entertained Paul and his shipwrecked companions for three days, till they found a more permanent place of residence; for they remained on the island for three months, till the stormy season had passed.

Pudens.title: Pudens
Pudens.summary: Bashful, a Christian at Rome, who sent his greetings to Timothy .

Quartus.title: Quartus
Quartus.summary: Fourth, a Corinthian Christian who sent by Paul his salutations to friends at Rome .

Raamah.title: Raamah
Raamah.summary: One of the sons of Cush .

Rachab.title: Rachab
Rachab.summary: '=Rahab, a name found in the genealogy of our Lord .

Rachel.title: Rachel
Rachel.summary: Ewe, “the daughter”, “the somewhat petulant, peevish, and self-willed though beautiful younger daughter” of Laban, and one of Jacob’s wives .

Rahab.title: Rahab
Rahab.summary: Insolence; pride, a poetical name applied to Egypt in ; ; , as “the proud one.” Rahab, (Heb.

Raham.title: Raham
Raham.summary: Merciful, one of the descendants of Caleb, the son of Hezron .

Ram.title: Ram
Ram.summary: The son of Hezron, and one of the ancestors of the royal line .

Ram.title: Ram
Ram.summary: One of the sons of Jerahmeel .

Ram.title: Ram
Ram.summary: A person mentioned in as founder of a clan to which Elihu belonged.

Reba.title: Reba
Reba.summary: Fourth, one of the Midianite chiefs slain by the Israelites in the wilderness .

Rebekah.title: Rebekah
Rebekah.summary: A noose, the daughter of Bethuel, and the wife of Isaac .

Rechab.title: Rechab
Rechab.summary: One of Ishbosheth’s “captains of bands” or leaders of predatory troops .

Rechab.title: Rechab
Rechab.summary: The father of Jehonadab, who was the father of the Rechabites (, ; ).

Reelaiah.title: Reelaiah
Reelaiah.summary: Thunder of the Lord, one of the princes who returned from the Exile ; called also Reelaiah .

Rehabiah.title: Rehabiah
Rehabiah.summary: Enlargement of the Lord, the son of Eliezer, and grandson of Moses .

Rehob.title: Rehob
Rehob.summary: The father of Hadadezer, king of Tobah .

Rehoboam.title: Rehoboam
Rehoboam.summary: He enlarges the people, the successor of Solomon on the throne, and apparently his only son.

Rehum.title: Rehum
Rehum.summary: One of “the children of the province” who returned from the Captivity ; the same as “Nehum” .

Rehum.title: Rehum
Rehum.summary: The “chancellor” of Artaxerxes, who sought to stir him up against the Jews  and prevent the rebuilding of the walls and the temple of Jerusalem.

Rei.title: Rei
Rei.summary: Friendly, one who maintained true allegiance to king David  when Adonijah rebelled.

Rekem.title: Rekem
Rekem.summary: One of the five Midianite kings whom the Israelites destroyed .

Rekem.title: Rekem
Rekem.summary: One of the sons of Hebron .

Remaliah.title: Remaliah
Remaliah.summary: Adorned by the Lord, the father of Pekah, who conspired successfully against Pekahiah (, , , , ; , , , ; 8:6).

Rephael.title: Rephael
Rephael.summary: Healed of God, one of Shemaiah’s sons.

Reuben.title: Reuben
Reuben.summary: Behold a son!, the eldest son of Jacob and Leah .

Reuel.title: Reuel
Reuel.summary: A son of Esau and Bashemath (, ; ).

Rezin.title: Rezin
Rezin.summary: Firm; a prince, a king of Syria, who joined Pekah (q.v.) in an invasion of the kingdom of Judah .

Rhesa.title: Rhesa
Rhesa.summary: Affection, son of Zorobabel, mentioned in the genealogy of our Lord .

Rhoda.title: Rhoda
Rhoda.summary: A rose, the damsel in the house of Mary, the mother of John Mark.

Rimmon.title: Rimmon
Rimmon.summary: A man of Beeroth , one of the four Gibeonite cities.

Riphath.title: Riphath
Riphath.summary: A crusher, Gomer’s second son , supposed to have been the ancestor of the Paphlagonians.

Rizpah.title: Rizpah
Rizpah.summary: Coal; hot stone, the daughter of Aiah, and one of Saul’s concubines.

Romamti-ezer.title: Romamti-ezer
Romamti-ezer.summary: Elevation of help, one of the sons of Heman, “the king’s seer in the words of God, to lift up the horn.” He was head of the “four-and-twentieth” course of singers .

Ruth.title: Ruth
Ruth.summary: A friend, a Moabitess, the wife of Mahlon, whose father, Elimelech, had settled in the land of Moab.

Sabtah.title: Sabtah
Sabtah.summary: Rest, the third son of Cush .

Sacar.title: Sacar
Sacar.summary: A son of Obed-edom the Gittite, and a temple porter .

Sadoc.title: Sadoc
Sadoc.summary: Just, mentioned in the genealogy of our Lord .

Salah.title: Salah
Salah.summary: A shoot, a descendant of Arphaxed ; called also Shelah .

Salathiel.title: Salathiel
Salathiel.summary: Whom I asked of God, the son of Jeconiah ; also called the son of Neri .

Salmon.title: Salmon
Salmon.summary: Shady; or Zalmon (q.v.), a hill covered with dark forests, south of Shechem, from which Abimelech and his men gathered wood to burn that city .

Salome.title: Salome
Salome.summary: The wife of Zebedee and mother of James and John , and probably the sister of Mary, the mother of our Lord .

Samson.title: Samson
Samson.summary: Of the sun, the son of Manoah, born at Zorah.

Sanballat.title: Sanballat
Sanballat.summary: Held some place of authority in Samaria when Nehemiah went up to Jerusalem to rebuild its ruined walls.

Sarah.title: Sarah
Sarah.summary: Princess, the wife and at the same time the half-sister of Abraham .

Saul.title: Saul
Saul.summary: The son of Kish (probably his only son, and a child of prayer, “asked for”), of the tribe of Benjamin, the first king of the Jewish nation.

Sceva.title: Sceva
Sceva.summary: An implement, a Jew, chief of the priests at Ephesus ; i.e., the head of one of the twenty-four courses of the house of Levi.

Seba.title: Seba
Seba.summary: One of the sons of Cush .

Secundus.title: Secundus
Secundus.summary: Second, a Christian of Thessalonica who accompanied Paul into Asia .

Segub.title: Segub
Segub.summary: The youngest son of Hiel the Bethelite.

Seir.title: Seir
Seir.summary: A Horite; one of the “dukes” of Edom .

Semei.title: Semei
Semei.summary: Mentioned in the genealogy of our Lord .

Sennacherib.title: Sennacherib
Sennacherib.summary: Sin (the god) sends many brothers, son of Sargon, whom he succeeded on the throne of Assyria (B.C.

Seorim.title: Seorim
Seorim.summary: Barley, the chief of the forth priestly course .

Serah.title: Serah
Serah.summary: Abundance; princess, the daughter of Asher and grand-daughter of Jacob ; called also Sarah (; R.V., “Serah”).

Sered.title: Sered
Sered.summary: Fear, one of the sons of Zebulun .

Sergius.title: Sergius Paulus
Sergius.summary: A “prudent man” (R.V., “man of understanding”), the deputy (R.V., “proconsul”) of Cyprus .

Serug.title: Serug
Serug.summary: Branch, the father of Nahor ; called Saruch in .

Seth.title: Seth
Seth.summary: Appointed; a substitute, the third son of Adam and Eve .

Sethur.title: Sethur
Sethur.summary: Hidden, one of the spies sent to search the Promised Land.

Shaashgaz.title: Shaashgaz
Shaashgaz.summary: Servant of the beautiful, a chief eunuch in the second house of the harem of king Ahasuerus .

Shadrach.title: Shadrach
Shadrach.summary: Aku’s command, the Chaldean name given to Hananiah, one of the Hebrew youths whom Nebuchadnezzar carried captive to Babylon (, ; 3:12-30).

Shallum.title: Shallum
Shallum.summary: The son of Jabesh, otherwise unknown.

Shallum.title: Shallum
Shallum.summary: Keeper of the temple vestments in the reign of Josiah .

Shallum.title: Shallum
Shallum.summary: One of the posterity of Judah .

Shalmaneser.title: Shalmaneser
Shalmaneser.summary: An Assyrian king , identified with Shalmaneser II.

Shamer.title: Shamer
Shamer.summary: A man of Asher ; called also Shamer (34).

Shamgar.title: Shamgar
Shamgar.summary: The Philistines from the maritime plain had made incursions into the Hebrew upland for the purposes of plunder, when one of this name, the son of Anath, otherwise unknown, headed a rising for the purpose of freeing the land from this oppression.

Shamir.title: Shamir
Shamir.summary: One of the sons of Michah .

Shammah.title: Shammah
Shammah.summary: One of David’s three mighty men .

Shammah.title: Shammah
Shammah.summary: One of David’s mighties ; called also Shammoth  and Shamhuth (27:8).

Sharar.title: Sharar
Sharar.summary: One of David’s heroes ; called also Sharar .

Sharezer.title: Sharezer
Sharezer.summary: (god) protect the king!, a son of Sennacherib, king of Assyria.

Shaul.title: Shaul
Shaul.summary: A king of Edom ; called Shaul in .

Shealtiel.title: Shealtiel
Shealtiel.summary: Asked for of God, father of Zerubbabel (, ; ).

Shebaniah.title: Shebaniah
Shebaniah.summary: A Levite appointed to blow the trumpet before the ark of God .

Shebaniah.title: Shebaniah
Shebaniah.summary: A priest who returned from the Captivity with Zerubbabel (; marg., or Shebaniah).

Shebna.title: Shebna
Shebna.summary: Tender youth, “treasurer” over the house in the reign of Hezekiah, i.e., comptroller or governor of the palace.

Shecaniah.title: Shecaniah
Shecaniah.summary: A priest to whom the tenth lot came forth when David divided the priests .

Shecaniah.title: Shecaniah
Shecaniah.summary: One of the priests who were set “to give to their brethren by courses” of the daily portion .

Shechem.title: Shechem
Shechem.summary: The son of Hamor the Hivite .

Shem.title: Shem
Shem.summary: A name; renown, the first mentioned of the sons of Noah .

Shemaiah.title: Shemaiah
Shemaiah.summary: A prophet in the reign of Rehoboam .

Shemariah.title: Shemariah
Shemariah.summary: One who joined David at Ziklag .

Shemeber.title: Shemeber
Shemeber.summary: Soaring on high, the king of Zeboiim, who joined with the other kings in casting off the yoke of Chedorlaomer.

Shemiramoth.title: Shemiramoth
Shemiramoth.summary: A Levite in the reign of Jehoshaphat .

Shemuel.title: Shemuel
Shemuel.summary: A prince of the tribe of Issachar .

Shephatiah.title: Shephatiah
Shephatiah.summary: A Benjamite who joined David at Ziklag .

Shephatiah.title: Shephatiah
Shephatiah.summary: A Simeonite prince in David’s time .

Shethar.title: Shethar
Shethar.summary: A star, a prince at the court of Ahasuerus .

Shethar-boznai.title: Shethar-boznai
Shethar-boznai.summary: Star of splendour, a Persian officer who vainly attempted to hinder the rebuilding of the temple (, ; 6:6, 13).

Shimeah.title: Shimeah
Shimeah.summary: A Benjamite, a descendant of Gibeon ; called also Shimeam (9:38).

Shimei.title: Shimei
Shimei.summary: A Levite of the family of Gershon .

Shimei.title: Shimei
Shimei.summary: A Benjamite of the house of Saul, who stoned and cursed David when he reached Bahurim in his flight from Jerusalem on the occasion of the rebellion of Absalom .

Shimshai.title: Shimshai
Shimshai.summary: The shining one, or sunny, the secretary of Rehum the chancellor, who took part in opposing the rebuilding of the temple after the Captivity (, , -23).

Shimri.title: Shimri
Shimri.summary: The father of one of the “valiant men” of David’s armies .

Shimri.title: Shimri
Shimri.summary: Assisted at the purification of the temple in the time of Hezekiah .

Shimrith.title: Shimrith
Shimrith.summary: The mother of Jehozabad, who murdered Joash ; called also Shimrith, a Moabitess .

Shinab.title: Shinab
Shinab.summary: Cooling, the king of Adamah, in the valley of Siddim, who with his confederates was conquered by Chedorlaomer .

Shiphrah.title: Shiphrah
Shiphrah.summary: Beauty, one of the Egyptian midwives .

Shobab.title: Shobab
Shobab.summary: One of David’s sons by Bathseheba .

Shobab.title: Shobab
Shobab.summary: One of the sons of Caleb , the son of Hezron.

Shobach.title: Shobach
Shobach.summary: Poured out, the “captain of the host of Hadarezer” when he mustered his vassals and tributaries from beyond “the river Euphrates” ; called also Shophach .

Shobal.title: Shobal
Shobal.summary: The second son of Seir the Horite; one of the Horite “dukes” .

Shobal.title: Shobal
Shobal.summary: One of the sons of Caleb, and a descendant of Hur (, ; , ).

Shua.title: Shua
Shua.summary: A daughter of Heber the Asherite .

Shuah.title: Shuah
Shuah.summary: One of Abraham’s sons by Keturah (; Chr.

Shuah.title: Shuah
Shuah.summary: A Canaanite whose daughter was married to Judah .

Shubael.title: Shubael
Shubael.summary: One of the descendants of Gershom, who had charge of the temple treasures in the time of David .

Shubael.title: Shubael
Shubael.summary: One of the sons of Heman; one of those whose duty it was to “lift up the horn” in the temple service ; called also Shubael (ver.

Sibbechai.title: Sibbechai
Sibbechai.summary: The Lord sustains, one of David’s heroes , general of the eighth division of the army (27:11).

Silas.title: Silas
Silas.summary: Wood, a prominent member of the church at Jerusalem; also called Silvanus.

Simeon.title: Simeon
Simeon.summary: The second son of Jacob by Leah .

Simeon.title: Simeon
Simeon.summary: One of the ancestors of Joseph .

Simon.title: Simon Peter
Simon.summary: Originally called Simon (=Simeon, i.e., “hearing”), a very common Jewish name in the New Testament.

Simon.title: Simon Zelotes
Simon.summary: One of the twelve apostles, called the Canaanite .

Simon.title: Simon
Simon.summary: One of the brothers of our Lord .

Sippai.title: Sippai
Sippai.summary: Extension, the son of the giant whom Sibbechai slew ; called also Sippai .

Sisera.title: Sisera (army captain)
Sisera.summary: The captain of Jabin’s army , which was routed and destroyed by the army of Barak on the plain of Esdraelon.

Sisera.title: Sisera
Sisera.summary: The ancestor of some of the Nethinim who returned with Zerubbabel .

So.title: So
So.summary: (Nubian, Sabako), an Ethiopian king who brought Egypt under his sway.

Sopater.title: Sopater
Sopater.summary: The father who saves, probably the same as Sosipater, a kinsman of Paul , a Christian of the city of Berea who accompanied Paul into Asia .

Sosthenes.title: Sosthenes
Sosthenes.summary: Safe in strength, the chief ruler of the synagogue at Corinth, who was seized and beaten by the mob in the presence of Gallio, the Roman governor, when he refused to proceed against Paul at the instigation of the Jews .

Stachys.title: Stachys
Stachys.summary: Spike; an ear of corn, a convert at Rome whom Paul salutes .

Stephanas.title: Stephanas
Stephanas.summary: Crown, a member of the church at Corinth, whose family were among those the apostle had baptized (; , ).

Stephen.title: Stephen
Stephen.summary: One of the seven deacons, who became a preacher of the gospel.

Susanna.title: Susanna
Susanna.summary: Lily, with other pious women, ministered to Jesus .

Susi.title: Susi
Susi.summary: The father of Gaddi, who was one of the twelve spies .

Syntyche.title: Syntyche
Syntyche.summary: Fortunate; affable, a female member of the church at Philippi, whom Paul beseeches to be of one mind with Euodias .

Tabeal.title: Tabeal
Tabeal.summary: Goodness of God, the father of one whom the kings of Syria and Samaria in vain attempted to place on the throne of Ahaz .

Tabrimon.title: Tabrimon
Tabrimon.summary: Good is Rimmon, the father of Benhadad, king of Syria .

Tahpenes.title: Tahpenes
Tahpenes.summary: The wife of Pharaoh, who gave her sister in marriage to Hadad the Edomite .

Talmai.title: Talmai
Talmai.summary: One of the Anakim of Hebron, who were slain by the men of Judah under Caleb .

Talmai.title: Talmai
Talmai.summary: A king of Geshur, to whom Absalom fled after he had put Amnon to death .

Tamar.title: Tamar
Tamar.summary: The daughter-in-law of Judah, to whose eldest son, Er, she was married .

Tamar.title: Tamar
Tamar.summary: A daughter of David , whom Amnon shamefully outraged and afterwards “hated exceedingly,” thereby illustrating the law of human nature noticed even by the heathen, “Proprium humani ingenii est odisse quem laeseris”, i.e., “It is the property of human nature to hate one whom you have injured.”

Tanhumeth.title: Tanhumeth
Tanhumeth.summary: Consolation, a Netophathite; one of the captains who supported Gedaliah .

Tartan.title: Tartan
Tartan.summary: One of Sennacherib’s messengers to Hezekiah .

Terah.title: Terah
Terah.summary: The wanderer; loiterer, for some unknown reason emigrated with his family from his native mountains in the north to the plains of Mesopotamia.

Tertullus.title: Tertullus
Tertullus.summary: A modification of “Tertius;” a Roman advocate, whom the Jews employed to state their case against Paul in the presence of Felix .

Thahash.title: Thahash
Thahash.summary: A badger, a son of Nahor, Abraham’s brother .

Theophilus.title: Theophilus
Theophilus.summary: Lover of God, a Christian, probably a Roman, to whom Luke dedicated both his Gospel  and the Acts of the Apostles (1:1).

Theudas.title: Theudas
Theudas.summary: Thanksgiving, referred to by Gamaliel in his speech before the council at Jerusalem .

Thomas.title: Thomas
Thomas.summary: Twin, one of the twelve (; , etc.).

Tiberius.title: Tiberius
Tiberius.summary: I.e., as known in Roman history, Tiberius Claudius Nero, only mentioned in .

Tibni.title: Tibni
Tibni.summary: Building of Jehovah, the son of Ginath, a man of some position, whom a considerable number of the people chose as monarch.

Timon.title: Timon
Timon.summary: Honouring, one of the seven deacons at Jerusalem .

Timotheus.title: Timotheus
Timotheus.summary: Honouring God, a young disciple who was Paul’s companion in many of his journeyings.

Tirhakah.title: Tirhakah
Tirhakah.summary: The last king of Egypt of the Ethiopian (the fifteenth) dynasty.

Tirzah.title: Tirzah
Tirzah.summary: The youngest of Zelophehad’s five daughters .

Titus.title: Titus
Titus.summary: Honourable, was with Paul and Barnabas at Antioch, and accompanied them to the council at Jerusalem , although his name nowhere occurs in the Acts of the Apostles.

Tobiah.title: Tobiah
Tobiah.summary: Pleasing to Jehovah, the “servant,” the “Ammonite,” who joined with those who opposed the rebuilding of Jerusalem after the Exile .

Togarmah.title: Togarmah
Togarmah.summary: A son of Gomer, and grandson of Japheth .

Toi.title: Toi
Toi.summary: A king of Hamath, who sent “Joram his son unto King David to salute him,” when he “heard that David had smitten all the host of Hadadezer” .

Tola.title: Tola
Tola.summary: A judge of the tribe of Issachar who “judged” Israel twenty-three years , when he died, and was buried in Shamir.

Trophimus.title: Trophimus
Trophimus.summary: A foster-child, an Ephesian who accompanied Paul during a part of his third missionary journey .

Tubal-cain.title: Tubal-cain
Tubal-cain.summary: The son of Lamech and Zillah, “an instructor of every artificer in brass and iron” (; R.V., “the forger of every cutting instrument of brass and iron”).

Tychicus.title: Tychicus
Tychicus.summary: Chance, an Asiatic Christian, a “faithful minister in the Lord” , who, with Trophimus, accompanied Paul on a part of his journey from Macedonia to Jerusalem .

Tyrannus.title: Tyrannus
Tyrannus.summary: Prince, a Greek rhetorician, in whose “school” at Ephesus Paul disputed daily for the space of two years with those who came to him .

Ucal.title: Ucal
Ucal.summary: The name of a person to whom Agur’s words are addressed .

Unni.title: Unni
Unni.summary: A Levite whom David appointed to take part in bringing the ark up to Jerusalem from the house of Obed-edom by playing the psaltery on that occasion .

Unni.title: Unni
Unni.summary: A Levite who returned with Zerubbabel from the Captivity .

Uriah.title: Uriah
Uriah.summary: A Hittite, the husband of Bathsheba, whom David first seduced, and then after Uriah’s death married.

Uriel.title: Uriel
Uriel.summary: A Kohathite ancestor of the prophet Samuel .

Uriel.title: Uriel
Uriel.summary: The chief of the Kohathites at the time when the ark was brought up to Jerusalem .

Uriel.title: Uriel
Uriel.summary: The father of Michaiah, one of Rehoboam’s wives, and mother of Abijah .

Uz.title: Uz (son of Aram)
Uz.summary: The son of Aram, and grandson of Shem .

Uz.title: Uz (son of Dishan)
Uz.summary: One of the Horite “dukes” in the land of Edom .

Uzal.title: Uzal
Uzal.summary: A wanderer, a descendant of Joktan , the founder apparently of one of the Arab tribes; the name also probably of the province they occupied and of their chief city.

Uzzah.title: Uzzah
Uzzah.summary: Strength, a son of Abinadab, in whose house the men of Kirjath-jearim placed the ark when it was brought back from the land of the Philistines .

Uzzi.title: Uzzi
Uzzi.summary: The son of Bukki, and a descendant of Aaron (, ; ).

Uzzi.title: Uzzi
Uzzi.summary: A son of Bela, and grandson of Benjamin .

Uzziah.title: Uzziah
Uzziah.summary: One of Amaziah’s sons, whom the people made king of Judah in his father’s stead .

Uzziah.title: Uzziah
Uzziah.summary: The father of Jehonathan, one of David’s overseers .

Vajezatha.title: Vajezatha
Vajezatha.summary: Purity; worthy of honour, one of Haman’s sons, whom the Jews slew in the palace of Shushan .

Vashti.title: Vashti
Vashti.summary: Beautiful, the queen of Ahasuerus, who was deposed from her royal dignity because she refused to obey the king when he desired her to appear in the banqueting hall of Shushan the palace .

Zabad.title: Zabad
Zabad.summary: One of David’s valiant men , the descendant of Ahlai, of the “children of Sheshan” (2:31).

Zabbai.title: Zabbai
Zabbai.summary: The father of Baruch, who “earnestly repaired” part of the wall of Jerusalem (; marg., “Zaccai”).

Zabdi.title: Zabdi
Zabdi.summary: Called “the Shiphmite,” one of David’s officers, who had charge of his vineyards .

Zaccai.title: Zaccai
Zaccai.summary: Pure, one whose “sons” returned with Zerubbabel to Jerusalem .

Zaccur.title: Zaccur
Zaccur.summary: Father of Shammua, who was one of the spies sent out by Moses .

Zaccur.title: Zaccur
Zaccur.summary: A son of Asaph, and chief of one of the courses of singers as arranged by David .

Zachariah.title: Zachariah
Zachariah.summary: Son of Jeroboam II., king of Israel.

Zadok.title: Zadok
Zadok.summary: A son of Ahitub, of the line of Eleazer , high priest in the time of David  and Solomon .

Zadok.title: Zadok
Zadok.summary: The father of Jerusha, who was wife of King Uzziah, and mother of King Jotham .

Zalmunna.title: Zalmunna
Zalmunna.summary: One of the two kings of Midian whom the “Lord delivered” into the hands of Gideon.

Zebadiah.title: Zebadiah
Zebadiah.summary: A Korhite porter of the Lord’s house .

Zebah.title: Zebah
Zebah.summary: Man-killer, or sacrifice, one of the two kings who led the vast host of the Midianites who invaded the land of Israel, and over whom Gideon gained a great and decisive victory .

Zebedee.title: Zebedee
Zebedee.summary: A Galilean fisherman, the husband of Salome (q.v.), and the father of James and John, two of our Lord’s disciples .

Zebudah.title: Zebudah
Zebudah.summary: Given, the wife of Josiah and mother of Jehoiakim .

Zebul.title: Zebul
Zebul.summary: Habitation, the governor of Shechem under Abimelech (, , ).

Zebulun.title: Zebulun
Zebulun.summary: Dwelling, the sixth and youngest son of Jacob and Leah .

Zechariah.title: Zechariah
Zechariah.summary: The father of Abijah, who was the mother of Hezekiah .

Zechariah.title: Zechariah
Zechariah.summary: One of the chiefs of the tribe of Reuben .

Zechariah.title: Zechariah
Zechariah.summary: A Levite who assisted at the bringing up of the ark from the house of Obededom .

Zedekiah.title: Zedekiah
Zedekiah.summary: The son of Chenaanah, a false prophet in the days of Ahab (, ; , ).

Zeeb.title: Zeeb
Zeeb.summary: The wolf, one of the two leaders of the great Midianite host which invaded Israel and was utterly routed by Gideon.

Zelek.title: Zelek
Zelek.summary: Cleft, an Ammonite; one of David’s valiant men .

Zelophehad.title: Zelophehad
Zelophehad.summary: First-born, of the tribe of Manasseh, and of the family of Gilead; died in the wilderness.

Zemira.title: Zemira
Zemira.summary: Vine-dresser, a Benjamite; one of the sons of Becher .

Zenas.title: Zenas
Zenas.summary: A disciple called “the lawyer,” whom Paul wished Titus to bring with him .

Zephaniah.title: Zephaniah
Zephaniah.summary: The son of Maaseiah, the “second priest” in the reign of Zedekiah, often mentioned in Jeremiah as having been sent from the king to inquire  regarding the coming woes which he had denounced, and to entreat the prophet’s intercession that the judgment threatened might be averted (, , ; 37:3; 52:24).

Zephaniah.title: Zephaniah
Zephaniah.summary: The father of Josiah, the priest who dwelt in Jerusalem when Darius issued the decree that the temple should be rebuilt .

Zerah.title: Zerah
Zerah.summary: A son of Tamar ; called also Zara .

Zeresh.title: Zeresh
Zeresh.summary: Star of Venus, the wife of Haman, whom she instigated to prepare a gallows for Mordecai .

Zeruah.title: Zeruah
Zeruah.summary: Stricken, mother of Jeroboam, the first king of the ten tribes .

Zerubbabel.title: Zerubbabel
Zerubbabel.summary: The seed of Babylon, the son of Salathiel or Shealtiel (; Zorobabel, ); called also the son of Pedaiah , i.e., according to a frequent usage of the word “son;” the grandson or the nephew of Salathiel.

Zeruiah.title: Zeruiah
Zeruiah.summary: Stricken of the Lord, David’s sister, and the mother of Abishai, Joab, and Asahel , who were the three leading heroes of David’s army, and being his nephews, they were admitted to the closest companionship with him.

Ziba.title: Ziba
Ziba.summary: Post; statue, “a servant of the house of Saul” , who informed David that Mephibosheth, a son of Jonathan, was alive.

Zidkijah.title: Zidkijah
Zidkijah.summary: The Lord is righteous, one who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah .

Zidon.title: Zidon
Zidon.summary: A fishery, a town on the Mediterranean coast, about 25 miles north of Tyre.

Ziha.title: Ziha
Ziha.summary: The name of a family of Nethinim .

Zillah.title: Zillah
Zillah.summary: Shadow, one of the wives of Lamech, of the line of Cain, and mother of Tubal-cain .

Zilpah.title: Zilpah
Zilpah.summary: Drooping, Leah’s handmaid, and the mother of Gad and Asher .

Zilthai.title: Zilthai
Zilthai.summary: One of the captains of the tribe of Manasseh who joined David at Ziklag .

Zimran.title: Zimran
Zimran.summary: Vine-dressers; celebrated, one of the sons of Abraham by Keturah .

Zippor.title: Zippor
Zippor.summary: A little bird, the father of Balak, king of Moab .

Zithri.title: Zithri
Zithri.summary: The Lord protects, a Levite, son of Uzziel .

Zoar.title: Zoar
Zoar.summary: Small, a town on the east or south-east of the Dead Sea, to which Lot and his daughters fled from Sodom .

Zohar.title: Zohar
Zohar.summary: The father of Ephron the Hittite .

Zoheth.title: Zoheth
Zoheth.summary: Snatching (?), one of the sons of Ishi .

Zophah.title: Zophah
Zophah.summary: Spreading out, a son of Helem , a chief of Asher.

Zophar.title: Zophar
Zophar.summary: Chirping, one of Job’s friends who came to condole with him in his distress (.

Zuph.title: Zuph
Zuph.summary: Honeycomb, a Kohathite Levite, ancestor of Elkanah and Samuel ; called also Zophai .

Zur.title: Zur
Zur.summary: One of the five Midianite kings whom the Israelites defeated and put to death .

Zuriel.title: Zuriel
Zuriel.summary: Rock of God, chief of the family of the Merarites  at the time of the Exodus.

Zurishaddai.title: Zurishaddai
Zurishaddai.summary: Rock of the Almighty, the father of Shelumiel, who was chief of the tribe of Simeon when Israel was encamped at Sinai .
